data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification).

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 passes cleanly — this is a strong chunk):
- [T1a] Prior art is considered before committing, and not blindly repackaged. The plan names the in-repo analogue (`local_label_intent`'s "ever-seen" set) AND external prior art (Unito/Exalate/FolderSync/Syncthing/ghas-jira-integration), then explains precisely WHY the naive repackaging fails: `local_label_intent` projects from raw event history, which `compact_ticket` collapses to a SNAPSHOT, re-resurrecting post-boundary removals. Confirmed in code: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/local_label_intent.py` scans CREATE/EDIT/TAG_DELTA/SNAPSHOT events for an ever-seen union — exactly the compaction-fragile projection the plan correctly diagnoses.
- [T1b] The novel element (moving provenance from a history-scan projection into a reducer-maintained `compiled_state` field) is justified vs the established approach with a concrete failure mode (compaction durability), and is framed as a Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging primitive ("build the durable primitive ONCE, shared by parent + links + future peers").
- [T1c] The load-bearing capability assertion is verified, not assumed. "`process_snapshot` restores it for free across compaction" holds: `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:515-519` shows `process_snapshot` copies every `compiled_state` key onto state. The forward-compat posture is consistent with the repo's existing handling.

ONE COACHING FINDING (strengthens the novel-pattern justification; not a blocker):

- [ ] Cite the closest IN-REPO precedent for the "reducer-maintained lazy map folded into `compiled_state`, restored across compaction" mechanism to convert the implicit novel-pattern framing into an explicit Rule-of-Three: `workflow_runs`/`workflow_steps` (epic a88f / WS-C1) already implement exactly this. `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:80-86` documents it: WORKFLOW_RUN/WORKFLOW_STEP events "folds them into ticket state as the lazy per-key maps workflow_runs / workflow_steps ... preserved in compiled_state, restored by process_snapshot." Naming this shows `managed_refs` is an ESTABLISHED reducer pattern (compaction-folded compiled_state map), which is a stronger anti-repackaging position than the external-tool citations alone — and pre-empts any "is this a novel architecture?" objection in later passes.
  - criteria: [T1]
  - location: plan "What" section — "A monotonic, compaction-surviving `managed_refs` provenance set maintained by the reducer in `compiled_state` (so `process_snapshot` restores it for free across compaction)"
  - evidence: `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:80-86` (WORKFLOW_RUN -> `workflow_runs` compiled_state-folded-and-restored precedent); the plan references external tools and `local_label_intent` but not this nearest structural precedent.
  - suggested_fix: (none — author's call on wording/placement)
